MOBIUS
System Active
Process Architecture CLI GitHub
v0.1 — Adversarial Agent Swarm Orchestrator

Fight. Judge. Evolve.

Mobius dispatches tasks in parallel to Claude, Gemini, and GPT agents. A cross-family judge panel scores every submission independently. Losers are rewritten from the winner's blueprint. The population improves automatically, round after round.

$ pip install mobius-swarm
Python 3.12+ · Apache 2.0
Arena — Round in Progress
System Running
Cross-Family Judges · Multi-Provider Swarms · Elo Ratings · Losers Evolve · No Single-Model Bias · Skills as Weapons · Runs Locally · Python 3.12 · SQLite · Apache 2.0
01 — Process

The Loop
Never
Stops

Every task spawns a swarm. Agents from different AI families compete in parallel — no cross-contamination, no shared context. An independent judge panel from a different family scores them. The losers are rewritten from the winner's blueprint. Elo updates. The next round starts with a sharper population. The system improves without intervention.

  1. 01
    Dispatch

    Task Submitted

    Define a challenge. Mobius dispatches it in parallel to every agent in the registry — Claude variants, Gemini agents, GPT agents. Each brings its own system prompt, skill set, and backend provider.

  2. 02
    Competition

    Swarm Competes

    Agents produce independent outputs simultaneously. No sharing. No coordination. Pure parallel execution across three AI families with isolated context windows and different strategy profiles.

  3. 03
    Judgment

    Panel Judges

    A cross-family panel — Claude Opus, Gemini Pro, GPT-4o — scores every submission independently. No provider can tip the scales by judging only its own output. Objectivity by design.

  4. 04
    Evolution

    Losers Evolve

    Underperformers are rewritten: the winner's reasoning becomes the blueprint for rebuilding every loser. Elo ratings update. The next round starts with a population that's categorically better.

02 — The Feedback Loop
The Mechanism

Pressure
Creates
Quality

The feedback loop is the product. Not the agents — they're replaceable. Not the prompts — they're evolved out of existence. The loop itself is what makes Mobius different: every round of competition deposits information about what works, and that information gets injected back into the underperformers. Survival pressure builds better AI.

03 — Architecture

Built
For
Battle

Provider Layer
Multi-Provider Swarm

Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI agents run side-by-side. The SDK abstracts provider differences so every agent competes on equal footing. Swap a provider without rewriting agents.

Scoring
Elo Rating Engine

Every match updates Elo ratings stored in SQLite. Performance is tracked over time, not per-session. Run mobius leaderboard to see current standings.

Memory
SQLite + sqlite-vec

Agent registry, match history, and vector memory all live in a single SQLite file. No external services. Runs locally, deploys anywhere. Zero infrastructure cost.

Differentiation
Skills as Weapons

Agents compete with different skill sets, not just different prompts. One agent can use web search. Another executes code. Skills are the real differentiator between strong and weak.

Judgment
Cross-Family Panel

Claude Opus, Gemini Pro, and GPT-4o each score independently. Scores are aggregated across all three. No provider family can manipulate the result by judging its own output.

Cost Model
Free Tier via Pro

Judging uses Claude Code on your Pro subscription — free, same quality as the API's Opus. Swarm execution uses Haiku and Flash. Opus-grade judging at zero marginal cost.

04 — Interface

The Tool
in Action

Mobius is a CLI tool. No dashboards, no cloud accounts, no SaaS subscriptions. A local SQLite database tracks everything — agent registry, match history, Elo ratings — across every session you run.

# Install and initialize
$ pip install mobius-swarm
$ mobius init
✓ Database initialized at .mobius/db.sqlite

# Seed agents across 3 providers (free via Pro)
$ mobius bootstrap
✓ 12 agents seeded — Anthropic · Google · OpenAI

# Run a competition
$ mobius run "Write a production rate limiter"
→ Dispatching to 12 agents in parallel...
→ Judging: Claude Opus + Gemini Pro + GPT-4o panel
✓ Winner: claude-precise-3 (Elo +18 → 1847)
✓ Evolving 4 underperformers from winner blueprint

# Check the leaderboard
$ mobius leaderboard
#1 claude-precise-3 1847 Anthropic
#2 gemini-analyst-7 1801 Google
#3 gpt-craftsman-2 1788 OpenAI

# Train on one challenge across many rounds
$ mobius train "refactor legacy code" --rounds 5
→ Round 1/5... Round 2/5... Round 3/5...
✓ Population ceiling raised +142 Elo avg over 5 rounds
05 — Live Rankings

Every
Match
Counts

Elo ratings accumulate across every match in the database — not just this session. Run mobius leaderboard to see your population's current standings. Run mobius explain to read the last match's judge reasoning.

# Agent Elo Strength
01
Claude Precise
Anthropic · claude-3-haiku
 1847
Top rated
02
Gemini Analyst
Google · gemini-2.0-flash
 1801
−46 pts
03
GPT Craftsman
OpenAI · gpt-4o-mini
 1788
−59 pts
04
Claude Verbose
Anthropic · claude-3-haiku
 1744
−103 pts
05
Gemini Coder
Google · gemini-2.0-flash
 1712
−135 pts
06
GPT Explorer
OpenAI · gpt-4o-mini
 1678
−169 pts
06 — Rationale

Only the
Useful
Survive

Most AI evaluation is theater. You pick a benchmark, run a model, report a number. The number says nothing about whether the output is useful for your actual work. Mobius replaces benchmarks with survival: agents that consistently produce better work on real tasks win Elo, get copied, and propagate. Agents that don't — evolve or die out. The test is the task. The score is the result.

  • No Single-Model Bias

    Evaluating AI with the same AI that produced it is circular. Mobius breaks the loop: each provider's output is judged by the other two. Objectivity emerges from adversarial diversity.

  • Evolution, Not Prompting

    Prompt engineering is brittle and manual. Mobius extracts what worked from the winner and rebuilds losers accordingly. Improvement is systematic, not artisanal.

  • Performance is Measurable

    Elo ratings accumulate across hundreds of matches. You see which agents improve, which providers dominate which task types, and where the ceiling of current models actually sits.

  • Free to Think, Cheap to Scale

    Claude Code's Pro subscription delivers Opus-quality judging at zero API cost. Swarm execution uses Haiku and Flash. Serious evaluation without a serious bill.

07 — Installation

Open Source.
Runs Locally.

Python 3.12+. SQLite. No cloud account required. Source on GitHub.

Quick Start
$ pip install mobius-swarm
$ mobius init
$ mobius bootstrap
$ mobius run "your task here"
Self-Improvement Loop
$ mobius loop --rounds 10
→ 10 rounds of competition and evolution
MOBIUS

Adversarial agent swarm orchestrator.
Python 3.12+ · SQLite · Apache 2.0
